title: Helping retail investors navigate decision fatigue on mobile
summary: This case study explores how I reframed in-app campaign discovery from passive feed browsing into an active, momentum-driven experience, helping investors prioritise and re-engage with an emphasis on retention.
outcomes: Defined a clear browse → shortlist → decide loop for campaign discovery
Introduced lightweight controls to save, hide, and filter campaigns on mobile
Created a scalable pattern set to support iterative testing and rollouts
+155% increase in campaign follows since release
Launched native app to >25,000 users in UK and Europe

sections.title: Design Challenge
body: Imagine this: You're a retail investor opening your crowdfunding app a few times a week. Dozens of campaigns are live, with new ones launching regularly. You want to make thoughtful decisions — and not miss the next big opportunity.

Over time, the volume starts to blur. Some campaigns catch your eye, but you're not ready to commit. Others feel familiar, but you can't quite remember whether you've reviewed them, or why you moved on.
A few days pass. You open the app again, unsure what's genuinely new and what you've already assessed. Gradually, the experience becomes mentally taxing.
The issue wasn't a lack of information. It was designing for continuity — helping investors focus, filter, and build confidence across repeated mobile sessions.
sections.leadIn: The experience needed to make it easy to:
identifiedItems: Scan quickly and understand what's new (or worth revisiting)
Shortlist promising campaigns without forcing a decision
Reduce noise by removing irrelevant campaigns from view
Return later and pick up where you left off
bodyEnd: In short: less browsing as a chore, more discovery as a confident, repeatable habit. I led the work across three core areas

sections.title: Action Taken
body: To shape the right solution, I led a four-week discovery phase combining behavioural analysis with 8 in-depth investor interviews.
sections.leadIn: The work focused on three core questions:
identifiedItems: How do investors navigate live campaigns?
How do they return across sessions?
bodyEnd: One insight in particular — "I don't want to see investments I'm not interested in" — opened up a new avenue to explore.

Investors didn't want to keep seeing opportunities they'd already ruled out. They didn't want to dig through irrelevant information. And when returning to the app, they wanted clarity on what to look at next.
Discovery wasn't about bookmarking for later. It was about filtering in the moment and progressing with confidence.

sections.title: Results
body: The outcome was a clearer, calmer discovery experience: the feed becomes a place to make progress, not just consume volume.

Investors can now externalise memory (save), reduce noise (hide), and narrow attention (filter) without breaking flow—supporting confident decisions over time instead of forcing them in a single session.

media.text: ...It just feels better. I can get rid of the ones I'm not into and focus on the good ones.

identifiedItems: A repeatable browse → shortlist → decide loop designed for short sessions
A lightweight control set that reduces decision fatigue without adding complexity
+155% increase in campaign follows since release
Launched native app to UK and Europe, growing user base to >25,000
4.1 out of 5 App Store rating reflecting improved user satisfaction
